Label: parameter
Definition: A data item involved in PBPK modelling.
Textual Definition: A data item involved in PBPK modelling.

Label: physiologically based pharmacokinetic model
Definition: A data representation model that predicts the absorption, distribution, metabolism, and excretion (ADME) of chemical substances in biological species based on physiological principles using mathematical modelling technique.
Textual Definition: A data representation model that predicts the absorption, distribution, metabolism, and excretion (ADME) of chemical substances in biological species based on physiological principles using mathematical modelling technique.

Label: whole body pbpk
Definition: A physiologically based pharmacokinetic model that contains an explicit representation of the organs most relevant to the ADME of the compound.
Textual Definition: A physiologically based pharmacokinetic model that contains an explicit representation of the organs most relevant to the ADME of the compound.

Label: perfusion limited model
Definition: A physiologically based pharmacokinetic model that assumes tissue concentration reaches equilibrium rapidly and uptake/elimination is limited by blood flow rate.
Textual Definition: A physiologically based pharmacokinetic model that assumes tissue concentration reaches equilibrium rapidly and uptake/elimination is limited by blood flow rate.

Label: physiological parameter
Definition: A parameter that provides the structural framework for a PBPK model and relates to the organism's physiology (e.g., body weight, organ volume, blood flow).
Textual Definition: A parameter that provides the structural framework for a PBPK model and relates to the organism's physiology (e.g., body weight, organ volume, blood flow).

Label: body mass index
Definition: A physiological parameter calculated as an individual's weight (kg) divided by the square of their height (m).
Textual Definition: A physiological parameter calculated as an individual's weight (kg) divided by the square of their height (m).

Label: bodyweight
Definition: A physiological parameter representing the mass or quantity of heaviness of an individual.
Textual Definition: A physiological parameter representing the mass or quantity of heaviness of an individual.

Label: height

Label: surface area

Label: physicochemical parameter
Definition: A parameter relating to the physical and chemical properties of a compound (e.g., MW, lipophilicity, solubility, pKa).
Textual Definition: A parameter relating to the physical and chemical properties of a compound (e.g., MW, lipophilicity, solubility, pKa).

Label: molecular weight
Definition: A physicochemical parameter representing the sum of the weights of all atoms in a molecule.
Textual Definition: A physicochemical parameter representing the sum of the weights of all atoms in a molecule.

Label: lipophillicity
Definition: A physicochemical parameter representing the affinity or tendency of a compound to dissolve in lipids or hydrophobic environments.
Textual Definition: A physicochemical parameter representing the affinity or tendency of a compound to dissolve in lipids or hydrophobic environments.

Label: distribution coefficient
Definition: A physicochemical parameter representing the logarithm of the ratio of concentrations of a compound in a mixture of two immiscible phases at equilibrium, specifically describing lipophilicity (LogD).
Textual Definition: A physicochemical parameter representing the logarithm of the ratio of concentrations of a compound in a mixture of two immiscible phases at equilibrium, specifically describing lipophilicity (LogD).

Label: dissolution in two phase system
Definition: A physicochemical parameter representing the ratio of a dissolved compound in a two-phase system (often used interchangeably with partition coefficient).
Textual Definition: A physicochemical parameter representing the ratio of a dissolved compound in a two-phase system (often used interchangeably with partition coefficient).

Label: logarithmic solubility
Definition: A physicochemical parameter representing the 10-based logarithm of a compound's solubility (typically in mol/L).
Textual Definition: A physicochemical parameter representing the 10-based logarithm of a compound's solubility (typically in mol/L).

Label: water solubility
Definition: A physicochemical parameter representing the solubility of a compound specifically in water.
Textual Definition: A physicochemical parameter representing the solubility of a compound specifically in water.

Label: acid dissociation constant
Definition: A physicochemical parameter representing the negative base-10 logarithm (pKa) of the acid dissociation constant (Ka).
Textual Definition: A physicochemical parameter representing the negative base-10 logarithm (pKa) of the acid dissociation constant (Ka).

Label: basic dissociation constant
Synonyms: pKb pK(b) pK(B) affinity constant, Base dissociation constant

Textual Definition: A physicochemical parameter representing the negative base-10 logarithm (pKb) of the base dissociation constant (Kb).

Label: physiological charge

Textual Definition: A physicochemical parameter representing the electrical charge of a molecule at physiological pH.

Label: hydrogen donor count

Label: rotatable bond count

Label: biochemical parameter

Textual Definition: A biochemical parameter representing the ratio of the concentration of a compound in a particular tissue to its concentration in plasma/blood at equilibrium.

Textual Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in stomach to plasma.

Textual Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in pancreas to plasma.

Label: liver plasma partition coefficient

Textual Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in liver to plasma.

Label: kidney plasma partition coefficient

Textual Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in kidney to plasma.

Label: muscle plasma partition coefficient
Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in muscle to plasma.
Textual Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in muscle to plasma.

Label: heart plasma partition coefficient
Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in heart to plasma.
Textual Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in heart to plasma.

Label: fat plasma partition coefficient
Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in fat (adipose tissue) to plasma.
Textual Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in fat (adipose tissue) to plasma.

Label: gonad plasma partition coefficient
Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in gonads to plasma.
Textual Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in gonads to plasma.

Label: skin plasma partition coefficient
Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in skin to plasma.
Textual Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in skin to plasma.

Label: bone plasma partition coefficient
Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in bone to plasma.
Textual Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in bone to plasma.

Label: spleen plasma partition coefficient
Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in spleen to plasma.
Textual Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in spleen to plasma.

Label: lung plasma partition coefficient
Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in lung to plasma.
Textual Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in lung to plasma.

Label: rich perfused plasma partition coefficient
Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in the lumped richly perfused compartment to plasma.
Textual Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in the lumped richly perfused compartment to plasma.

Label: poor perfused plasma partition coefficient
Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in the lumped poorly perfused compartment to plasma.
Textual Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in the lumped poorly perfused compartment to plasma.

Label: skin stratum corneum partition coefficient
Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in the stratum corneum to the rest of the skin.
Textual Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in the stratum corneum to the rest of the skin.

Label: air plasma partition coefficient
Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in air to plasma.
Textual Definition: A partition coefficient representing the ratio of compound concentration in air to plasma.

Label: skin diffusion coefficient
Definition: A diffusion coefficient representing the diffusion of a compound across the skin.
Textual Definition: A diffusion coefficient representing the diffusion of a compound across the skin.

Label: human serum albumin
Definition: A material entity representing distribution-related protein (most abundant in human plasma) that binds various compounds.
Textual Definition: A material entity representing distribution-related protein (most abundant in human plasma) that binds various compounds.

Label: blood:plasma ratio
Definition: A biochemical parameter representing the ratio of compound concentration in whole blood to that in plasma.
Textual Definition: A biochemical parameter representing the ratio of compound concentration in whole blood to that in plasma.

Label: catalytic rate constant
Definition: A biochemical parameter quantifying enzymatic reaction velocity (kcat), representing the maximum number of substrate molecules converted per active site per unit time.
Textual Definition: A biochemical parameter quantifying enzymatic reaction velocity (kcat), representing the maximum number of substrate molecules converted per active site per unit time.

Label: phase I metabolism
Definition: A metabolism process involving the conversion of exogenous substances to more polar metabolites, often via oxidation, reduction, or hydrolysis (e.g., by CYPs).
Textual Definition: A metabolism process involving the conversion of exogenous substances to more polar metabolites, often via oxidation, reduction, or hydrolysis (e.g., by CYPs).

Label: phase II
Definition: A metabolism process involving the conjugation of phase I products (or parent compounds) to hydrophilic moieties to facilitate excretion.
Textual Definition: A metabolism process involving the conjugation of phase I products (or parent compounds) to hydrophilic moieties to facilitate excretion.

Label: phase III
Definition: A metabolism/transport process involving the further processing and excretion of conjugated metabolites (often involving efflux transporters).
Textual Definition: A metabolism/transport process involving the further processing and excretion of conjugated metabolites (often involving efflux transporters).

Label: first pass metabolism
Definition: A metabolism process occurring at a specific location (e.g., liver, gut wall) after administration but before reaching systemic circulation, reducing the concentration of active compound.
Textual Definition: A metabolism process occurring at a specific location (e.g., liver, gut wall) after administration but before reaching systemic circulation, reducing the concentration of active compound.

Label: maximum rate in intestine
Definition: A maximum rate (Vmax) of an enzyme catalysed reaction for a compound in the intestine when the enzyme is saturated with substrate.
Textual Definition: A maximum rate (Vmax) of an enzyme catalysed reaction for a compound in the intestine when the enzyme is saturated with substrate.

Label: Michaelis constant  intestine
Definition: Michaelis constant representing the substrate concentration at which the reaction rate in the intestine is half of Vmax (Km).
Textual Definition: Michaelis constant representing the substrate concentration at which the reaction rate in the intestine is half of Vmax (Km).

Label: maximum rate in liver
Definition: A maximum rate (Vmax) of an enzyme catalysed reaction for a compound in the liver when the enzyme is saturated with substrate.
Textual Definition: A maximum rate (Vmax) of an enzyme catalysed reaction for a compound in the liver when the enzyme is saturated with substrate.

Label: Michaelis constant  liver
Definition: Michaelis constant representing the substrate concentration at which the reaction rate in the liver is half of Vmax (Km).
Textual Definition: Michaelis constant representing the substrate concentration at which the reaction rate in the liver is half of Vmax (Km).

Label: gut metabolism
Definition: A metabolism process specifically occurring within the gut.
Textual Definition: A metabolism process specifically occurring within the gut.

Label: dissociation constant
Definition: A biochemical parameter (Kd) measuring the affinity of a compound for its binding partner (e.g., receptor, protein).
Textual Definition: A biochemical parameter (Kd) measuring the affinity of a compound for its binding partner (e.g., receptor, protein).

Label: transporter
Definition: A material entity representing protein responsible for the import or export of compounds across cell membranes.
Textual Definition: A material entity representing protein responsible for the import or export of compounds across cell membranes.

Label: oatp
Definition: A transporter family (Organic Anion Transporting Polypeptides) involved in transporting organic anions.
Textual Definition: A transporter family (Organic Anion Transporting Polypeptides) involved in transporting organic anions.

Label: abcc3
Definition: A transporter (ATP-binding cassette subfamily C member 3) involved in bile metabolism and transport.
Textual Definition: A transporter (ATP-binding cassette subfamily C member 3) involved in bile metabolism and transport.

Label: abcc2
Definition: A transporter (ATP-binding cassette subfamily C member 2) involved in transporting various molecules across cell membranes.
Textual Definition: A transporter (ATP-binding cassette subfamily C member 2) involved in transporting various molecules across cell membranes.

Label: degradation rate constant
Definition: A biochemical parameter (kdeg) representing the rate constant for the degradation of a metabolizing enzyme or transporter.
Textual Definition: A biochemical parameter (kdeg) representing the rate constant for the degradation of a metabolizing enzyme or transporter.

Label: inhibition constant
Definition: A biochemical parameter (Ki) representing the inhibition constant for a specific enzyme or transporter interaction.
Textual Definition: A biochemical parameter (Ki) representing the inhibition constant for a specific enzyme or transporter interaction.

Label: renal excretion
Definition: An excretion process involving the elimination of compounds and metabolites primarily through urine via the kidneys.
Textual Definition: An excretion process involving the elimination of compounds and metabolites primarily through urine via the kidneys.

Label: hepatic excretion
Definition: An excretion process involving the elimination of compounds and metabolites via the liver (often into bile).
Textual Definition: An excretion process involving the elimination of compounds and metabolites via the liver (often into bile).

Label: fecal excretion
Definition: An excretion process involving the elimination of compounds and metabolites through feces.
Textual Definition: An excretion process involving the elimination of compounds and metabolites through feces.

Label: exhalation
Definition: An excretion process involving the elimination of volatile compounds and metabolites through respiration.
Textual Definition: An excretion process involving the elimination of volatile compounds and metabolites through respiration.

Label: sweat excretion
Definition: An excretion process involving the elimination of compounds and metabolites through sweat.
Textual Definition: An excretion process involving the elimination of compounds and metabolites through sweat.

Label: hair excretion
Definition: An excretion process involving the elimination and deposition of compounds and metabolites into hair.
Textual Definition: An excretion process involving the elimination and deposition of compounds and metabolites into hair.

Label: clearance rate
Definition: A biochemical parameter representing the volume of biological fluid cleared of a compound per unit time.
Textual Definition: A biochemical parameter representing the volume of biological fluid cleared of a compound per unit time.

Label: fecal clearance rate
Definition: A clearance rate specifically quantifying elimination via feces.
Textual Definition: A clearance rate specifically quantifying elimination via feces.

Label: sweat clearance rate
Definition: A clearance rate specifically quantifying elimination via sweat.
Textual Definition: A clearance rate specifically quantifying elimination via sweat.

Label: urinary clearance rate
Definition: A clearance rate specifically quantifying elimination via urine.
Textual Definition: A clearance rate specifically quantifying elimination via urine.

Label: biliary clearance rate
Definition: A clearance rate specifically quantifying elimination via bile.
Textual Definition: A clearance rate specifically quantifying elimination via bile.

Label: hepatic clearance rate
Definition: A clearance rate specifically quantifying elimination via the liver (metabolism and/or biliary excretion).
Textual Definition: A clearance rate specifically quantifying elimination via the liver (metabolism and/or biliary excretion).

Label: intrinsic clearance rate
Definition: A clearance rate representing the inherent ability of an organ or tissue to eliminate a compound, independent of blood flow or binding.
Textual Definition: A clearance rate representing the inherent ability of an organ or tissue to eliminate a compound, independent of blood flow or binding.

Label: fecal elimination rate constant
Definition: A biochemical parameter representing the rate constant for elimination via fecal excretion.
Textual Definition: A biochemical parameter representing the rate constant for elimination via fecal excretion.

Label: renal elimination rate constant
Definition: A biochemical parameter representing the rate constant for elimination via renal (urinary) excretion.
Textual Definition: A biochemical parameter representing the rate constant for elimination via renal (urinary) excretion.

Label: biliary elimination
Definition: An elimination process involving the irreversible transfer of compound or metabolites from plasma to bile via hepatocytes.
Textual Definition: An elimination process involving the irreversible transfer of compound or metabolites from plasma to bile via hepatocytes.

Label: route of exposure
Definition: A parameter designating the body part or method through which exposure is introduced.
Textual Definition: A parameter designating the body part or method through which exposure is introduced.

Label: oral exposure
Definition: A route of exposure via the mouth.
Textual Definition: A route of exposure via the mouth.

Label: dermal exposure
Definition: A route of exposure via the skin.
Textual Definition: A route of exposure via the skin.

Label: inhalation exposure
Definition: A route of exposure via inhalation into the lungs.
Textual Definition: A route of exposure via inhalation into the lungs.

Label: intravenous bolus exposure
Definition: A route of exposure involving rapid injection directly into a vein.
Textual Definition: A route of exposure involving rapid injection directly into a vein.

Label: intravenous infusion exposure
Definition: A route of exposure involving continuous administration directly into a vein over time.
Textual Definition: A route of exposure involving continuous administration directly into a vein over time.

Label: intramuscular exposure
Definition: A route of exposure involving injection into a muscle.
Textual Definition: A route of exposure involving injection into a muscle.

Label: intraarterial exposure
Definition: A route of exposure involving administration directly into an artery.
Textual Definition: A route of exposure involving administration directly into an artery.

Label: intrathecal exposure
Definition: A route of exposure involving administration into the spinal canal.
Textual Definition: A route of exposure involving administration into the spinal canal.

Label: subcutaneous exposure
Definition: A route of exposure involving administration under the skin.
Textual Definition: A route of exposure involving administration under the skin.

Label: nasal exposure
Definition: A route of exposure via the nasal passages.
Textual Definition: A route of exposure via the nasal passages.

Label: transdermal exposure
Definition: A route of exposure involving absorption through the skin, typically from a patch.
Textual Definition: A route of exposure involving absorption through the skin, typically from a patch.

Label: ocular exposure
Definition: A route of exposure via the eye.
Textual Definition: A route of exposure via the eye.

Label: output parameter
Definition: A parameter representing a result or prediction generated by the PBPK model.
Textual Definition: A parameter representing a result or prediction generated by the PBPK model.

Label: peak concentration
Definition: An output parameter representing the highest concentration (Cmax) of a compound achieved after exposure.
Textual Definition: An output parameter representing the highest concentration (Cmax) of a compound achieved after exposure.

Label: maximum steady state concentration
Definition: An output parameter representing the maximum concentration (Cmax,ss) achieved at steady state during repeated dosing.
Textual Definition: An output parameter representing the maximum concentration (Cmax,ss) achieved at steady state during repeated dosing.

Label: maximum time
Definition: An output parameter representing the time (Tmax) required to reach the peak concentration.
Textual Definition: An output parameter representing the time (Tmax) required to reach the peak concentration.

Label: area under curve
Definition: An output parameter representing the integral of the concentration-time curve (AUC), indicating total exposure.
Textual Definition: An output parameter representing the integral of the concentration-time curve (AUC), indicating total exposure.

Label: area under curve 0 to t
Definition: An area under curve parameter calculated from time zero to a specific time point t (AUC0-t).
Textual Definition: An area under curve parameter calculated from time zero to a specific time point t (AUC0-t).

Label: area under curve last
Definition: An area under curve parameter calculated from time zero to the time of the last measurable concentration (AUClast).
Textual Definition: An area under curve parameter calculated from time zero to the time of the last measurable concentration (AUClast).

Label: area under curve 0 to infinity
Definition: An area under curve parameter extrapolated from time zero to infinity (AUC0-inf), representing total exposure after a single dose.
Textual Definition: An area under curve parameter extrapolated from time zero to infinity (AUC0-inf), representing total exposure after a single dose.

Label: area under moment curve
Definition: An output parameter calculated as the integral of the product of time and concentration versus time (AUMC).
Textual Definition: An output parameter calculated as the integral of the product of time and concentration versus time (AUMC).

Label: mean residence time
Definition: An output parameter representing the average time a compound resides in the body (MRT).
Textual Definition: An output parameter representing the average time a compound resides in the body (MRT).

Label: half-life
Definition: An output parameter representing the time required for the compound concentration to decrease by half (t1/2).
Textual Definition: An output parameter representing the time required for the compound concentration to decrease by half (t1/2).

Label: bioavailability
Definition: An output parameter representing the rate and extent to which an active compound is absorbed and becomes available at the site of action or systemic circulation.
Textual Definition: An output parameter representing the rate and extent to which an active compound is absorbed and becomes available at the site of action or systemic circulation.

Label: relative bioavailability
Definition: A bioavailability parameter comparing the rate and extent of absorption of a test compound to a reference compound.
Textual Definition: A bioavailability parameter comparing the rate and extent of absorption of a test compound to a reference compound.

Label: mean absorption time
Definition: An output parameter representing the average time for a compound to be absorbed into systemic circulation (MAT).
Textual Definition: An output parameter representing the average time for a compound to be absorbed into systemic circulation (MAT).

Label: mean dissolution time
Definition: An output parameter representing the average time for a compound to dissolve in the body (MDT).
Textual Definition: An output parameter representing the average time for a compound to dissolve in the body (MDT).

Label: bioequivalence
Definition: An output parameter indicating comparable bioavailability between test and reference formulations under similar conditions.
Textual Definition: An output parameter indicating comparable bioavailability between test and reference formulations under similar conditions.

Label: absolute bioavailability
Definition: A bioavailability parameter comparing the amount reaching systemic circulation after non-IV administration to the amount available after IV administration.
Textual Definition: A bioavailability parameter comparing the amount reaching systemic circulation after non-IV administration to the amount available after IV administration.

Label: elimination phase
Definition: An elimination process phase describing the period where removal of the compound from the body predominates.
Textual Definition: An elimination process phase describing the period where removal of the compound from the body predominates.

Label: absorption phase
Definition: An absorption process phase describing the period where the compound moves from the administration site into systemic circulation.
Textual Definition: An absorption process phase describing the period where the compound moves from the administration site into systemic circulation.

Label: amount in urine
Definition: The amount of compound in the urine compartment.
Textual Definition: The amount of compound in the urine compartment.

Label: amount in feces
Definition: The amount of compound in the feces compartment.
Textual Definition: The amount of compound in the feces compartment.

Label: cumulative amount in urine
Definition: An output parameter representing the total amount of compound excreted in urine over a specific time period.
Textual Definition: An output parameter representing the total amount of compound excreted in urine over a specific time period.

Label: cumulative amount in feces
Definition: An output parameter representing the total amount of compound excreted in feces over a specific time period.
Textual Definition: An output parameter representing the total amount of compound excreted in feces over a specific time period.

Label: steady state area under curve
Definition: An area under curve parameter calculated over a dosing interval at steady state (AUCss).
Textual Definition: An area under curve parameter calculated over a dosing interval at steady state (AUCss).

Label: min organ concentration
Definition: An output parameter representing the minimum concentration of a compound in a specific organ after exposure.
Textual Definition: An output parameter representing the minimum concentration of a compound in a specific organ after exposure.

Label: max organ concentration
Definition: An output parameter representing the maximum concentration of a compound in a specific organ after exposure.
Textual Definition: An output parameter representing the maximum concentration of a compound in a specific organ after exposure.

Label: median organ concentration
Definition: An output parameter representing the median concentration of a compound in a specific organ after exposure.
Textual Definition: An output parameter representing the median concentration of a compound in a specific organ after exposure.

Label: percentile 2.5 cplasma
Definition: A plasma concentration parameter representing the 2.5th percentile of the plasma concentration distribution in a population simulation.
Textual Definition: A plasma concentration parameter representing the 2.5th percentile of the plasma concentration distribution in a population simulation.

Label: percentile 97.5 cplasma
Definition: A plasma concentration parameter representing the 97.5th percentile of the plasma concentration distribution in a population simulation.
Textual Definition: A plasma concentration parameter representing the 97.5th percentile of the plasma concentration distribution in a population simulation.

Label: tolerable daily intake
Definition: An output parameter (or exposure limit) estimating the amount of a substance that can be ingested daily over a lifetime without appreciable health risk (TDI).
Textual Definition: An output parameter (or exposure limit) estimating the amount of a substance that can be ingested daily over a lifetime without appreciable health risk (TDI).

Label: reconstructed exposure
Definition: An output parameter estimating the amount of compound exposure based on internal body measurements.
Textual Definition: An output parameter estimating the amount of compound exposure based on internal body measurements.

Label: estimated daily intake
Definition: An output parameter estimating the amount of a compound consumed daily (EDI).
Textual Definition: An output parameter estimating the amount of a compound consumed daily (EDI).

Label: biomonitoring equivalent
Definition: An output parameter representing the concentration of a chemical (or metabolite) in a biological medium consistent with exposure guidance values (BE).
Textual Definition: An output parameter representing the concentration of a chemical (or metabolite) in a biological medium consistent with exposure guidance values (BE).

Label: reference dose
Definition: An output parameter (or exposure limit) estimating the daily oral or dermal exposure likely to be without appreciable risk over a lifetime (RfD).
Textual Definition: An output parameter (or exposure limit) estimating the daily oral or dermal exposure likely to be without appreciable risk over a lifetime (RfD).

Label: acceptable daily intake
Definition: An output parameter (or exposure limit) estimating the amount of a substance in food/water that can be consumed daily without appreciable health risk (ADI).
Textual Definition: An output parameter (or exposure limit) estimating the amount of a substance in food/water that can be consumed daily without appreciable health risk (ADI).

Label: reference human data set
Definition: A data set containing the physiological parameters representing a standard human, often defined by specific characteristics like 70 kg body weight.
Textual Definition: A data set containing the physiological parameters representing a standard human, often defined by specific characteristics like 70 kg body weight.

Label: biological matrix concentration
Definition: An output parameter representing the total concentration of a compound in a specified biological matrix.
Textual Definition: An output parameter representing the total concentration of a compound in a specified biological matrix.

Label: concentration in hair
Definition: The total concentration of compound in the hair compartment.
Textual Definition: The total concentration of compound in the hair compartment.

Label: concentration in nail
Definition: A biological matrix concentration specifically representing the total concentration of compound in the nail compartment.
Textual Definition: A biological matrix concentration specifically representing the total concentration of compound in the nail compartment.

Label: concentration in teeth
Definition: A biological matrix concentration specifically representing the total concentration of compound in the teeth compartment.
Textual Definition: A biological matrix concentration specifically representing the total concentration of compound in the teeth compartment.

Label: concentration in blood
Definition: The total concentration of compound in the blood compartment.
Textual Definition: The total concentration of compound in the blood compartment.

Label: concentration in urine
Definition: A biological matrix concentration specifically representing the total concentration of compound in the urine compartment.
Textual Definition: A biological matrix concentration specifically representing the total concentration of compound in the urine compartment.

Label: permeability limited pbpk
Definition: A physiologically based pharmacokinetic model that considers the permeability across membranes as a limiting factor for compound distribution.
Textual Definition: A physiologically based pharmacokinetic model that considers the permeability across membranes as a limiting factor for compound distribution.

Label: maximum rate of basolateral transporter in vitro
Definition: Maximum rate (Vmax) of transport via a basolateral transporter, measured in vitro.
Textual Definition: Maximum rate (Vmax) of transport via a basolateral transporter, measured in vitro.

Label: Michaelis constant baso
Definition: Michaelis constant representing the substrate concentration at half-maximal velocity (Km) for a basolateral transporter.
Textual Definition: Michaelis constant representing the substrate concentration at half-maximal velocity (Km) for a basolateral transporter.

Label: maximum rate of apical transporter in vitro
Definition: A maximum rate (Vmax) of an enzyme catalysed reaction for a compound across the apical membrane of an organ, measured in vitro, when the enzyme is saturated with substrate.
Textual Definition: A maximum rate (Vmax) of an enzyme catalysed reaction for a compound across the apical membrane of an organ, measured in vitro, when the enzyme is saturated with substrate.

Label: Michaelis constant apical
Definition: Michaelis constant representing the substrate concentration at half-maximal velocity (Km) for an apical transporter.
Textual Definition: Michaelis constant representing the substrate concentration at half-maximal velocity (Km) for an apical transporter.

Label: influx constant
Definition: A biochemical parameter (kin) describing the rate constant for compound movement into a cell or compartment.
Textual Definition: A biochemical parameter (kin) describing the rate constant for compound movement into a cell or compartment.

Label: efflux rate constant
Definition: A biochemical parameter (kefflux) describing the rate constant for compound movement out of a cell or compartment.
Textual Definition: A biochemical parameter (kefflux) describing the rate constant for compound movement out of a cell or compartment.

Label: plasma space
Definition: A physiological parameter representing the volume occupied by plasma within a tissue or the body.
Textual Definition: A physiological parameter representing the volume occupied by plasma within a tissue or the body.

Label: endosomal space compartment
Definition: A compartment representing the endosomal space within cells.
Textual Definition: A compartment representing the endosomal space within cells.

Label: interstitial space
Definition: A physiological parameter representing the fluid-filled space between blood vessels and cells.
Textual Definition: A physiological parameter representing the fluid-filled space between blood vessels and cells.

Label: effective permeability
Definition: A biochemical parameter (Peff) representing the rate at which a compound passes through a biological barrier (e.g., intestinal wall).
Textual Definition: A biochemical parameter (Peff) representing the rate at which a compound passes through a biological barrier (e.g., intestinal wall).

Label: population pbpk
Definition: A physiologically based pharmacokinetic model approach used to characterize interindividual variability within a population.
Textual Definition: A physiologically based pharmacokinetic model approach used to characterize interindividual variability within a population.

Label: top-down pbpk
Definition: A physiologically based pharmacokinetic model approach primarily based on fitting observed clinical data, often empirical.
Textual Definition: A physiologically based pharmacokinetic model approach primarily based on fitting observed clinical data, often empirical.

Label: bottom-up pbpk
Definition: A physiologically based pharmacokinetic model approach built mechanistically using in vitro and physiological data.
Textual Definition: A physiologically based pharmacokinetic model approach built mechanistically using in vitro and physiological data.

Label: one-compartment pbpk
Definition: A physiologically based pharmacokinetic model that treats the entire body as a single, kinetically homogenous unit (central compartment).
Textual Definition: A physiologically based pharmacokinetic model that treats the entire body as a single, kinetically homogenous unit (central compartment).

Label: central compartment
Definition: A compartment in a multi-compartment model representing plasma and highly perfused tissues where distribution is rapid.
Textual Definition: A compartment in a multi-compartment model representing plasma and highly perfused tissues where distribution is rapid.

Label: peripheral compartment
Definition: A compartment in a multi-compartment model representing tissues where compound distribution is slower compared to the central compartment.
Textual Definition: A compartment in a multi-compartment model representing tissues where compound distribution is slower compared to the central compartment.

Label: elimination
Definition: A biological process involving the irreversible removal of a compound from the body, encompassing metabolism and excretion.
Textual Definition: A biological process involving the irreversible removal of a compound from the body, encompassing metabolism and excretion.

Label: linear elimination
Definition: An elimination process that follows first-order kinetics (rate proportional to concentration).
Textual Definition: An elimination process that follows first-order kinetics (rate proportional to concentration).

Label: non-linear elimination
Definition: An elimination process that does not follow first-order kinetics (e.g., saturable processes).
Textual Definition: An elimination process that does not follow first-order kinetics (e.g., saturable processes).

Label: lifestage
Definition: A physiological parameter representing distinct phases of an individual's life (e.g., infant, adult, geriatric).
Textual Definition: A physiological parameter representing distinct phases of an individual's life (e.g., infant, adult, geriatric).

Label: fetus lifestage
Definition: A lifestage parameter representing the unborn offspring from the postembryonic stage until birth.
Textual Definition: A lifestage parameter representing the unborn offspring from the postembryonic stage until birth.

Label: infant lifestage
Definition: A lifestage parameter representing a very young child, typically under one year of age.
Textual Definition: A lifestage parameter representing a very young child, typically under one year of age.

Label: toddler lifestage
Definition: A lifestage parameter representing a young child, typically between one and three years old.
Textual Definition: A lifestage parameter representing a young child, typically between one and three years old.

Label: child lifestage
Definition: A lifestage parameter representing individuals typically between 3 and 11 years old.
Textual Definition: A lifestage parameter representing individuals typically between 3 and 11 years old.

Label: teenager lifestage
Definition: A lifestage parameter representing individuals typically between eleven and fifteen years old.
Textual Definition: A lifestage parameter representing individuals typically between eleven and fifteen years old.

Label: adolescent lifestage
Definition: A lifestage parameter representing the transitional stage between teenager and adulthood (approx. 15-18 years).
Textual Definition: A lifestage parameter representing the transitional stage between teenager and adulthood (approx. 15-18 years).

Label: adult lifestage
Definition: A lifestage parameter representing individuals typically from eighteen to thirty years old.
Textual Definition: A lifestage parameter representing individuals typically from eighteen to thirty years old.

Label: middle age lifestage
Definition: A lifestage parameter representing individuals typically from thirty to forty-five years old.
Textual Definition: A lifestage parameter representing individuals typically from thirty to forty-five years old.

Label: old age lifestage
Definition: A lifestage parameter representing individuals typically from forty-five to sixty years old (or older, definitions vary).
Textual Definition: A lifestage parameter representing individuals typically from forty-five to sixty years old (or older, definitions vary).

Label: pediatric lifestage
Definition: A lifestage parameter representing individuals typically below fifteen years of age.
Textual Definition: A lifestage parameter representing individuals typically below fifteen years of age.

Label: geriatric lifestage
Definition: A lifestage parameter representing older adults, often considered 60/65 years and older.
Textual Definition: A lifestage parameter representing older adults, often considered 60/65 years and older.

Label: brain pbpk
Definition: A physiologically based pharmacokinetic model specifically built to predict compound concentrations in the brain.
Textual Definition: A physiologically based pharmacokinetic model specifically built to predict compound concentrations in the brain.

Label: cerebrospinal fluid volume
Definition: A volume parameter representing the total volume of cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) compartment in the spinal region.
Textual Definition: A volume parameter representing the total volume of cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) compartment in the spinal region.

Label: cranial cerebrospinal fluid volume
Definition: A volume parameter representing the total volume of cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) compartment in the cranial region.
Textual Definition: A volume parameter representing the total volume of cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) compartment in the cranial region.

Label: brain mass
Definition: A physiological parameter representing the mass of the brain.
Textual Definition: A physiological parameter representing the mass of the brain.

Label: blood brain barrier compartment
Definition: A barrier compartment representing the blood-brain barrier (BBB).
Textual Definition: A barrier compartment representing the blood-brain barrier (BBB).

Label: spinal cerebrospinal fluid flow rate
Definition: A bodily fluid flow rate parameter representing the flow rate of cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) compartment in the spinal region.
Textual Definition: A bodily fluid flow rate parameter representing the flow rate of cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) compartment in the spinal region.

Label: cranial cerebrospinal fluid flow rate
Definition: A bodily fluid flow rate parameter representing the flow rate of cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) compartment in the cranial region.
Textual Definition: A bodily fluid flow rate parameter representing the flow rate of cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) compartment in the cranial region.
